k" now swaps to "log" on screen, so my coinage gets caught automatically from here on.

One flaw worth naming: the plural came out wrong — *"its notebooks"* became *"its log"*, not *"its logs"*. The swapper replaces every form of a banned word with the single replacement, so plurals and past tenses land in the wrong form. That's pre-existing, and it affects every row in the list, not just mine.

for verb, noun for noun. Then the ending carries: "copies" becomes "moves", "notebooks" becomes "logs".

Leave it blank when the swap is a change of wording, when either side is a phrase, or when the two sides are different kinds of word — blank rows swap to the plain replacement, which is the old behavior.

Blank is the safe default: a wrong `y` invents words ("shipped" would become "doned").

inter warning on the file (an extra blank line at the end).

I see you changed "inserted" to "insert" on the absorb row. That's the right call and it matters: the marked path takes only the first word, so "absorbing" now becomes "including"… no — it becomes "placing", from "place". Either way the row's first word is what carries, and "insert" being a plain verb keeps the whole row consistent.

. A pointer-only page earns its keep only if it's cheaper than reading what it points at — this one costs a rewrite every time anything moves, and di's `always.md` already names both word-list homes correctly.

So: delete it, or fix it? Deleting removes the thing that keeps rotting. Fixing keeps the map but re-arms the trap. I'd delete — say the word and I'll do it, or tell me to fix instead.

One judgment call: the build-opener sits alone in a corner, not in a row with the others, so matching it is optional. The other five clearly should agree.

ts#L32).

I can't preview (rule 4), so the exact split is your eye's call — I'd apply 0/4, you look, we adjust. One caveat I can't rule out without seeing it: the flex-centered pills (all/any, tag pill, switcher) center their text and may not move with a padding change; if they stay low, I'll nudge their segments the same 2px. Say go and I'll do the heights plus the 0/4 padding, then you check.

**Next up (proposed in [handoff.md](ji/notes/work/handoff.md#L5)):** dropping loose files already works; the gap is folders — a dropped folder is ignored today. Two questions I'd want answered before starting: how deep to go (top level only, or every folder within), and whether to keep bare filenames or carry the folder path so same-named files don't collide.

**Two questions I need answered before building** (both in the proposal): do the deep-inside files and the folder stand-in get the chosen tags, and should folder stand-ins show as their own table row or stay hidden?
